title: pholistoma membranaceum
intro: Pholistoma membranaceum, also known as the California coastal violet or popcorn flower, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and Baja California. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Pholistoma membranaceum have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections, and have also been found to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Herbal preparations of Pholistoma membranaceum can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
